observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the LCE agreed, to ensure that:

The LDRRMO shall include all the unexpended prior year balances in its LDRRMIP and use the format specified in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002;

The Municipal Accountant shall prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and submit on or before 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to the COA Audit Team of the LGU and provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements using the formats provided in Annex B and Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 respectively; and 

The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain registries for LDRRMF.

Reconciliation of records between the Municipal Accounting Office and the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer was not initiated.

The Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was not accurately prepared and corresponding disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements were not adequately provided. 

Lacking personnel to assist the Municipal Budget Officer since she is also the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require:

the Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for setting-up the RPT and SET Receivables; and

the Municipal Accountant to ensure that (i) the correct basis is used to book up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, (ii) properly record the transactions pertaining to the collections of property taxes, and (iii) analyze the entries provided in order to rectify the noted variances of the affected accounts.
observations.reason:
No certified list of property owners/ taxpayer’s name and their respective property tax payable was prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant and to the audit team.

Wanting the mandatory list to be prepared by the Acting Municipal Treasurer, correct basis for the setting up of the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year could not be established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately send collection letters to the delinquent property owners and provide a copy of their payments to this Office, and prepare the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of the Real Property Tax and post the same at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the local government unit concerned; and 

We likewise recommended that the legislative officials, in consultation with the Acting Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to draw up plans to strategize and strengthen the collection drive beyond the required posting and sending of notices.
observations.reason:
Collection Letters and Notice of Delinquency were not submitted to the audit team as proof of its compliance.

No SB resolution was prepared to strengthen the LGU’s collection drive.

Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern were not prepared; hence the number of hired JO workers was not based from the actual needs per office to supplement the regular employee.

Vacant positions remain unfilled due to lacking applicants

Assessment of each JO worker was not conducted prior to the renewal of contract.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to:
clearly identify the reconciling items in the BRS for the 13 bank accounts and draw the necessary JEV to recognize all reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the books of accounts;
regularly submit the mandatory financial reports and the supporting documents within the reglementary period; and
The Municipal Treasurer to post the balances of the Cash in Bank- LCCA in the cashbook, with proper segregation of the sources of funds; and coordinate with the depository bank to ensure the prompt delivery of the monthly bank statements or its equivalent to facilitate the timely reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts and return the amount of ₱3,800.00 to the SEF bank account.
The Municipal Accountant, during the Exit Conference on the Results of CY 2024 Audit conducted on May 30, 2025, accepted the audit observations and promised to take immediate actions pertaining to the provided audit recommendations.
The Audit Team will closely monitor the compliance of the management since the same observations were noted referencing to AAR 2023-2, 2021-2, and 2019-2, indicating deliberate and continued disregard of the corrective adjustments recommended by the audit team.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
revisit the Annual Audit Report for CY 2022 and 2023 and immediately comply the necessary adjusting entries;
prepare JEV to correct erroneous entry to Road Networks and CIP accounts; and
facilitate the submission of the DV’s and its supporting documents.
No dissenting opinion was provided by the management and all agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
The audit team will closely monitor the compliance of the Municipality on this audit recommendations as this is also a reiteration of the calendar year’s 2023-5, 2022-3, 2021-3, and 2019-4 audit recommendations which remained unimplemented to date.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The Municipal Engineer to fast track the completion/acquisition of the development projects, and moving forward, to ensure that all development projects are completed as of yearend;
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure accuracy of amounts of the 20% DF presented in the AIP and thoroughly evaluate and assess the projects that are included in the 20% DF for feasibility of its implementation and prepared supplemental AIP for any changes made.
The Municipal Accountant to urgently submit lacking DV together with complete supporting documents.
Management expressed its full commitment to take immediate action in complying with the audit recommendations.
Close monitoring on the management’s compliance pertaining to the audit recommendations will be made by the audit team as related recommendations remain unimplemented referencing to AAR 2023-7, 2022-7, and 2021-7.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare a revised LDRRMF Utilization Report duly validated and reconciled with the records of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer, and be submitted to the audit team within the reglementary period and the same be posted in the DILG’s Full Disclosure Policy Portal.   Also, provide correct balances on a per year basis of the Unexpended LDRRMF to the LDRRM Officer;
the LDRRM Officer to include all the unexpended prior year balances in its LDRRMIP duly indicated the source year it pertains.  Further, the LDRRM Council to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the activities funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the STF; and
the local financial managers to refrain from charging LDRRMO and other programs administrative expenses to the LDRRMF.
Management will comply with the audit recommendations.
Related audit recommendations remain unimplemented anchoring to AAR 2023-8, and 2022-12.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed, to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the yearend balance of the Advances for Payroll account, and moving forward, to record accurately the cash advances and liquidations.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed, to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the year-end balance of the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account, and moving forward, to record accurately the cash advances and liquidations.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed, to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

require all collectors to prepare and submit the RAAF incorporating therein the received, issued and cancelled accountable forms with and without face value; and 

impose internal control measures for the immediate remittance of collections.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Ensure that taxes withheld for payment to contractors on infrastructure projects are based on gross amount and upon recognition of the Construction in Progress;

Ensure that all tax remittances to BIR are duly supported with monthly return and the verified Monthly Alphalist of Payees (MAP); and

Remit all taxes withheld within the reglementary period to avoid possible imposition of penalty/surcharge.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed, to direct the:

BAC Secretariat to submit within the reglementary period all perfected contracts so that timely auditorial review be conducted for the detection and correction of errors;

BAC Chairperson to demand from the contractor concerned to submit the identified lacking supporting documents, and moving forward, to ensure complete documents prior to the perfection of contract agreement; and

Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers prior to its payment.
We recommended, and the LCE agreed, to spearhead the preparation and the submission of the AAPSI CY 2022 and prior years with much urgency.

We recommended that the LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Analyze the unreconciled balance and determine the national government agency that granted the fund transfer, and if necessary, draw a correcting journal entry; and

Exert much effort in confirming the outstanding obligations from the NGA concerned and issue demand letter to the accountable officers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to require the BAC its strict compliance to the relevant provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and ensure that the contract be submitted within five days from perfection thereof.
We recommended that the LCE to submit an explanation why the procurement of COVID-19-related goods and services were undertaken through cash advances and not through emergency procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to perform the following: 

duly prepare the monthly and quarterly pre-closing trial balances and timely submit the same not later than the 20th day after the end of the month; and

prepare and maintain the complete books of original and final entry, including the subsidiary schedules, and submit the same upon every submission of trial balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a special account, complete with the special account subsidiary ledger, trial balance and financial statements, for the LGU’s water system operation.

We recommended that the LCE require:

the Municipal Assessor to submit up-to-date Assessment Roll to the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the former to immediately update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card;

the Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for setting-up the RPT and SET Receivables; and

the Municipal Accountant to ensure that (i) the correct basis is used to book up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, (ii) properly record the transactions pertaining to the collections of property taxes, and (iii) analyze the entries provided in order to rectify the noted variances of the affected accounts. 

Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant should always follow the correct accounting entries in order to reflect the correct amounts of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
No certified list of property owners/taxpayer’s name and their respective property tax payable was prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant and to the audit team. 




Wanting the mandatory list to be prepared by the Acting Municipal Treasurer, correct basis for the setting up of the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year could not be established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reinstate its obligation by drawing a correcting journal entry, if such was still unliquidated as of this writing; and moving forward, to ensure that only upon liquidation that the Municipality’s obligation to liquidate will be extinguished; 

Analyze the unreconciled balance and determine the national government agency that granted the fund transfer, and if necessary, draw a correcting journal entry; and

Exert much effort in confirming the outstanding obligations from the NGA concerned and issue demand letter to the accountable officers.

Subsidiary Ledgers were not maintained for the detailed monitoring of specific fund transfers particularly those fund transfers granted in 2013 and prior years. 




No confirmation letters were issued to various national government agencies to validate the accuracy and existence of the Municipality’s obligations.

He further argued that the perfected contracts were not submitted within the reglementary period due to lack of manpower that focuses on the processing of the supporting documents. However, he committed to do their best to address the noted deficiency.

the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.
We recommended that the LCE and the Sangguniang Bayan fast track the enactment and the subsequent implementation of the updated Municipal Revenue Code.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 49 of GAA FY 2021 which mandates that the net take home pay should not be lower than ₱5,000.00.
